title: Aster spithamaeus
taxonomy: Aster spithamaeus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Aster, which comprises over 180 species of daisy-like plants. The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family, and it consists of over 23,000 species worldwide. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other members of the Asteraceae family. Aster spithamaeus is native to the mountainous regions of eastern Asia, adding to its unique classification within this family.

intro: The Aster spithamaeus, also known as Spiny-leaved Aster or Small White Aster, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aster spithamaeus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aster spithamaeus is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, often used to treat fever, cough, and sore throats, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
